key_insights.insight: To assess an idea's viability, apply the 'so what' test, explaining why it matters in one sentence.
key_insights.quote: Tell yourself your idea and then ask, well, so what? And if you cannot explain why you would want to do that or why anybody should care in one sentence, it's not a good enough idea.

key_insights.insight: The key to sales is building trust and aligning your moral conviction with the product or service you're selling.
key_insights.quote: I couldn't sell something if I didn't think it was great... if I love something, I could sell it to anybody. So I guess it's more about aligning your moral conviction with whatever it is that you are then selling.

key_insights.insight: Exceptional customer service means being pleasant, patient, and entirely focused on the customer's needs and experience, not your own.
key_insights.quote: You gotta always be pleasant, right? I mean, you have to be patient. You have to be totally, absolutely focused on your customer... It was about them. It was not about me.

key_insights.insight: Hands-on experience builds confidence and allows you to step in and manage any role within your company if needed.
key_insights.quote: I'm a real believer of hands-on training... the confidence that comes with learning and perfecting a skill and being able to do it yourself... There's a real confidence in it.

key_insights.insight: Gratitude is a key business and life philosophy - be grateful that customers choose to support you, as they are essentially providing your paycheck.
key_insights.quote: I am grateful that people come to me to buy something or they trust me with their face right... they could be sponsoring so many other people but they're sponsoring me and isn't that so generous of them

key_insights.insight: Radically new ideas require association with existing models to facilitate user adoption.
key_insights.quote: if you're trying to create something new very often you have to relate it to something existing and people can more easily understand what you're talking about because you can say oh it's like this

key_insights.insight: Continual learning through the scanning of information and cross-pollination of ideas enhances creativity.
key_insights.quote: I scan everything, right? Whether or not I'm really interested in it, I got newsletters coming out of my eyeballs and I just kind of scan and I'll click on things... I think from doing that, I find new ideas.

actionable_advice.advice: Apply the 'so what' test to new ideas.
actionable_advice.how_to: State the idea, then ask 'so what?' If you cannot explain why the idea matters in one concise sentence, it may not be worthwhile.

actionable_advice.advice: Prioritize objective feedback to maintain trust
actionable_advice.how_to: Be willing to tell customers when work or services are substandard, which earns trust and strengthens long-term relationships.

actionable_advice.advice: Focus your communication on customer needs
actionable_advice.how_to: Learn to listen, understand their unstated desires and give them what they ask for, to become trusted advisors.

memorable_stories: Uma Thurman helping a 12-year-old acne patient open her locker at Bliss spa.
Flying back to Brooklyn on the Concorde from a business meeting in Paris and riding a Coke-soaked subway during the West Indian Day Parade.
The story of soap & glory bottom shelves
contrarian_claims: Entrepreneurs don't need business plans - passion and hands-on experience often lead to greater success.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - How I Built 5 Multi-Million Dollar Companies: Marcia Kilgore
